interpretation: The promoted XFEM branch remains numerically stable from the validated 200 mm closure point through 250 and 300 mm under mixed-control arc length. Because the effective kinematics are still small-strain shifted-Heaviside, these larger amplitudes are frontier/stability evidence, not the final finite-rotation physics certificate. The next formulation to implement is corotational XFEM before TL/UL.

cases.label: 200 mm promoted
cases.large_amplitude_recommendation: corotational-XFEM is the next required large-amplitude extension

cases.label: 250 mm small-strain frontier
cases.large_amplitude_recommendation: Use small-strain XFEM only as the calibrated 200 mm local-model closure baseline. For larger amplitudes, implement corotational-XFEM first because it removes rigid-rotation bias while preserving the current small-strain concrete and cohesive laws; migrate to UL only after the interface traction/jump measures and material history are made objective.

cases.label: 300 mm small-strain frontier
cases.large_amplitude_recommendation: Use small-strain XFEM only as the calibrated 200 mm local-model closure baseline. For larger amplitudes, implement corotational-XFEM first because it removes rigid-rotation bias while preserving the current small-strain concrete and cohesive laws; migrate to UL only after the interface traction/jump measures and material history are made objective.
